Prunus amygdalus is a deciduous tree native to the Middle East and Mediterranean region, known for its edible nuts and medicinal properties. taxonomy: Prunus × persicoamygdala is a hybrid species resulting from the cross between Prunus persica (peach) and Prunus dulcis (almond). It is a member of the Rosaceae family, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. The hybrid Prunus × persicoamygdala is primarily cultivated for its edible kernel, which is rich in oil and has various nutritional benefits. As a hybrid, Prunus × persicoamygdala is not a naturally occurring species in the wild and requires careful cultivation and maintenance. Through its classification, Prunus × persicoamygdala is recognized as a distinct species within the Prunus genus.
